name: Orbit Notes
oneLineDescription: A lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items.
whatItIs: Orbit Notes is a lightweight team workspace for capturing meeting notes, decisions, and action items. It helps small teams turn messy conversations into clear next steps. The primary use case is a team lead creates a note during or after a meeting, captures key decisions, assigns action items to teammates, and everyone can later search, filter, and track progress.
coreUserFlows: A team lead creates a note during or after a meeting, captures key decisions, assigns action items to teammates.
Team members search, filter, and track progress on action items.
Users sign up creating a new team or join an existing team via invite.
Team leads manage team membership by inviting new members and changing roles.

dataModel.purpose: Stores team member accounts with authentication and role information
Stores team workspace information and settings
Stores project containers for organizing notes
Stores meeting notes with rich text content and metadata
Stores tasks extracted from notes with assignment and completion tracking

crossCuttingPrinciples: Three roles with increasing permissions: Viewer (read-only), Contributor (create/edit), Team Lead (full management).
Single-team workspaces where users belong to exactly one team and all data access is scoped to the user's team.
A team must always have at least one Team Lead and cannot exist without members.
Rich text support is limited to headings, bold, italic, strikethrough, bullet lists, and numbered lists only.
specSummary: Orbit Notes is a focused team collaboration tool designed to transform meeting conversations into actionable outcomes. Built on Next.js 14 with Supabase backend, it centers around a three-tier permission system where Viewers can read, Contributors can create and edit content, and Team Leads manage the workspace. The core workflow involves creating notes within projects, extracting action items from those notes, and tracking progress through completion. Each user belongs to exactly one team, ensuring clean data isolation and simplified access control. The platform supports rich text editing with basic formatting, keyword search across all team content, and an activity timeline showing recent team actions. Project-based organization helps teams categorize their notes logically, while the action item system enables assignment and tracking of tasks derived from meetings. Team Leads have additional privileges to manage membership, invite new users, and control project lifecycles. The system deliberately excludes complex features like real-time collaboration, file attachments, or multi-team membership to maintain simplicity and focus on the core use case of turning meeting discussions into clear next steps.
